templates.description: Compress a draft response into caveman mode: drop articles, filler, pleasantries, hedging. Preserve all technical substance. Insert clarity exceptions for security and irreversible actions.

zed_layer.name: coding-guidelines
zed_layer.description: Enforce Andrej Karpathy's four coding behavioral principles before, during, and after implementation. Think Before Coding (surface assumptions, present alternatives), Simplicity First (minimum code, no speculative features), Surgical Changes (touch only what you must, match existing style), Goal-Driven Execution (define verifiable success criteria, loop until verified). Use when writing or reviewing code, before implementing a feature, or when auditing a diff for over-engineering.

templates.description: Assess a coding task against four behavioral principles before implementation. Surfaces assumptions, simplicity risks, scope creep warnings, and success criteria.
Generate constrained implementation directives from the assessment. Produces file-level guardrails, forbidden patterns, and style matching rules.
Verify an implementation or diff against all four principles. Produces a violations report, compliance scores, and corrective recommendations.

guidelines-apply.j2: visibility nested under contract (spec says top-level)
guidelines-assess.j2: energy_cap nested under contract (spec says top-level)

zed_layer.name: condenser-continuation
zed_layer.description: Continuation skill for resuming hKask condenser implementation work after context reset. Restores session state, prioritizes remaining tasks, verifies build status, and composes a structured continuation plan. Works with any hKask-supported inference engine (Ollama, Fireworks, DeepInfra, etc.).

templates.description: Distill session context into essential facts for condenser continuation: what was done, what remains, key files, and decisions. Inference-agnostic — does not depend on any specific LLM backend.
Rank remaining condenser tasks by priority (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW), identify blockers, and determine the immediate next action. Considers inference engine availability and graceful degradation.
Dispatch verification commands (cargo check, build, clippy, MCP handshake test) and collect results. Reports build health status.
Assemble the final continuation document from restored context, prioritized tasks, and verification results. Redacts sensitive data and formats as structured markdown.

zed_layer.name: constraint-forces
zed_layer.description: Classify constraints by force type (Prohibition, Guardrail, Guideline, Evidence, Hypothesis) to decide which can be relaxed and which are inviolable. Maps directly to Magna Carta P1–P4 enforcement levels. Use when deciding whether a constraint can be relaxed, when constraints conflict, or when the user asks 'can I change this rule?'

templates.description: Classify a statement or constraint into its force type and map to Magna Carta enforcement levels. Determines whether the constraint is relaxable and at what cost.
Resolve a conflict between two constraints by ranking their force types against each other. Higher force types dominate lower ones. Produces a winner, resolution strategy, and rationale.

top_defects: constraint-forces-classify.j2: energy_cap nested under contract (spec says top-level)
constraint-forces-classify.j2: visibility nested under contract (spec says top-level)

zed_layer.name: deep-module
zed_layer.description: Module design discipline based on John Ousterhout's 'A Philosophy of Software Design.' Apply the deletion test to evaluate whether a module deserves to exist: delete the callers — if complexity reappears, extract. Delete the module — if complexity vanishes, don't create it. Enforces depth (high benefit/cost ratio), interface minimalism (≤7 public functions), and dependency direction.

templates.description: Assess module depth: count public interface items, estimate behavior complexity, compute depth score, classify as Deep/Adequate/Shallow/ Very Shallow. Identify interface cost drivers and behavior gaps.

Execute the deletion test in both directions: caller's perspective (complexity reappears?) and module's perspective (complexity vanishes?). Produce a definitive keep/extract/don't create recommendation.

Design a deep module interface from deletion test results. Minimize public surface, hide information, design for caller mental model, unify config and errors. Produce a module specification with ≤7 public functions.

top_defects: deep-module-assess.j2: energy_cap nested under contract (spec says top-level)
deep-module-assess.j2: visibility nested under contract (spec says top-level)

zed_layer.name: diagnose
zed_layer.description: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Spec-anchored: every diagnosis maps to functional requirements. Reproduce, anchor, hypothesise, instrument, fix, regression-test, verify. Use when user says 'diagnose this' or 'debug this', reports a bug, says something is broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression.

templates.description: Build a feedback loop for the bug. Evaluate repro strategies, select the fastest deterministic signal, and confirm the bug reproduces before hypothesising.
Generate 3-5 ranked falsifiable hypotheses for the bug's root cause. Each hypothesis must state a prediction. Present to user before testing.
Instrument the code with targeted probes mapped to specific hypotheses. Change one variable at a time. Use tagged diagnostic logs or breakpoints.
Apply fix with regression test (before the fix). Verify original repro no longer reproduces. Clean up instrumentation. Write post-mortem.

top_defects: diagnose-fix.j2: energy_cap nested under contract (spec says top-level)
diagnose-fix.j2: visibility nested under contract (spec says top-level)
diagnose-hypothesise.j2: energy_cap nested under contract (spec says top-level)

zed_layer.name: document-update
zed_layer.description: Systematic documentation corpus maintenance workflow. Activates when user says "update docs", "documentation sweep", "align specs", "fix metadata", or "audit documentation". Wraps the hkask-mcp-spec server's 5-tool surface into a 7-task document-specific workflow: inventory classification, metadata alignment, writing quality gate, cross-corpus coherence, spec-code drift resolution, archive/consolidation, and portal refresh. Follows deep-module discipline (Ousterhout): ≤7 public operations, each doing one thing well. The deletion test: if you delete this skill, does the complexity of document updates reappear scattered across agent prompts? If yes, the skill earns its existence.

templates.description: Walk the docs/ corpus, extract YAML frontmatter from every .md file, classify lifecycle state against MDS_SCAFFOLD §3 state machine (Draft → Active → Deprecated → Superseded → Removed), flag stale status, missing/deprecated mds_categories, and references to removed/superseded subsystems. Produce corpus_inventory.yaml.

Apply surgical edits to YAML frontmatter on flagged documents: migrate ddmvss_categories to mds_categories (5-category taxonomy), fix stale status values, add missing audience/mds_categories fields, update last_updated to edit date. Metadata-only — do not touch document body content.

Invoke spec/require/writing-quality on specification documents. Assess 4 dimensions (Hopper/Lovelace/Schriver/Gentle). Flag documents scoring below 3/4. Apply structural discipline (Statement→Evidence→Diagram→Implications), add missing citations, split long sentences, replace passive voice. Use replica_compose for revision prose in Curator voice.

Invoke spec/graph/coherence on the corpus. Fix category gaps, incomplete specs, and cross-reference violations. Use spec/graph/query to trace reference chains. Persist each fix via condenser_persist as episodic memory triple.

Resolve spec-code drift items per curation decisions. For spec_ahead: add minimal stubs with FocusingAssumption annotations. For code_ahead: update spec to reflect actual method names/types. For divergent: apply type-alias or spec-update resolution. Follow Graydon Hoare pattern: types model domain truths.

Move deprecated/superseded documents to docs/archive/YYYY-MM-DD-<label>/. Git rm removed documents. Update all cross-references in active documents. Remove formerly/previously-known-as annotations. Git history is the archive of record.

Update docs/README.md portal to reflect current corpus: verify every listed document exists, add missing entries, remove archived entries, update MDS category tags. Update architecture master index. Verify every document reachable from portal in ≤2 clicks.

zed_layer.name: essentialist
zed_layer.description: General-purpose recursive eliminative interrogation. Enforces 'always take away, never add' through a 3-gate challenge loop (Exist → Surface → Contract) the actor must survive before any artifact can be committed. Use when the user says 'simplify this,' 'what can be deleted,' 'audit complexity,' 'strip this module,' 'is this necessary,' or 'essentialist review.'

templates.description: Orchestrate the 3-gate eliminative interrogation loop in either autonomous (agent evaluates and reduces without pause) or advisory (agent recommends, human accepts/rejects/overrides per gate) mode. Branch on pass/fail per gate, classify every finding by constraint-force (Prohibition → required, Guideline → suggested), escalate to human on retry exhaustion (3 max), abort on zero-delta completion. Delegates to deep-module (G1, G2) and coding-guidelines (G3) templates.

artifact.description: The artifact to interrogate (module, function, trait, type, or interface)

code_context.description: Surrounding code: callers, dependencies, module structure

mode.description: autonomous = agent evaluates/reduces without pause; advisory = agent presents findings, awaits human accept/reject/override per gate

max_rounds.description: Maximum full G1→G2→G3 rounds before escalation

max_retries_per_gate.description: Agent-level retries per gate before escalating to human

scope.description: Initial scope: 'auto' (full artifact), 'public', 'internal', or explicit path

elimination_report.description: Full report: deletions per gate, constraint-force breakdown (required vs suggested), essentialism score, escalation flag, human decisions (if advisory)

surviving_artifact.description: The artifact after all gates pass (may be reduced or empty)

top_defects: essentialist-flow.j2: energy_cap nested under contract (spec says top-level)
essentialist-flow.j2: visibility nested under contract (spec says top-level)

dimension_scores.description: Per-dimension cosine distance scores from replica_compare

exemplar_context.description: Retrieved exemplar passages for qualitative reference

zed_layer.name: grill-me
zed_layer.description: Intensely interrogate the user about a topic using Socratic questioning. Tests deep understanding through escalating difficulty, probes gaps in knowledge, and challenges assumptions. Use when the user says 'grill me about X' or wants their knowledge stress-tested.

templates.description: Generate interrogation questions at the appropriate difficulty level, evaluate answers, and decide whether to escalate, hold, or re-probe.
Synthesize final assessment with per-area ratings (Solid/Partial/Gap) and prioritized study recommendations.
Decide whether to escalate difficulty, hold, or re-probe based on answer quality ratio.

zed_layer.description: Continuation prompt for session handoffs. Captures what was done, what remains, key decisions, and recommended next steps so a new agent session can pick up where the previous one left off. Use when starting a new session that needs to continue work from a prior session, or when creating a handoff document before ending a session.

templates.description: Compress session context into a structured summary with purpose, progress, decisions, and current state. Reduces verbose conversation history to essential facts.
Identify and catalog artifacts by path or URL reference. Detect and flag sensitive data (API keys, tokens, PII) for redaction.
Analyze session content and suggest relevant skills the next agent should invoke. Extract open questions and risks for the next session.
Assemble the final handoff document from all gathered sections. Apply redaction, format as structured markdown, and write to the OS temp directory.

handoff-artifacts.j2: visibility nested under contract (spec says top-level)

zed_layer.name: improv
zed_layer.description: Composable interaction grammar for hKask agents. Five improv modes (Plussing, Yes And, Yes But, Freestyling, Riffing) provide constructive-by-default communication protocols for dual-presence chat and kata coaching loops. Use when agents need structured collaborative escalation, creative problem-solving, or constructive filtering of contributions.

templates.description: Full improv mode selection and application cycle.
Plussing (Catmull) — Extract agreeable components from a contribution, silently discard the remainder, and build constructively on selected seeds. Never explicitly negate.
Yes And — Accept the whole contribution and extend it with a novel, additive layer. Extension must be additive, not substitutive.
Yes But — Accept the whole contribution and append a constraint or redirect that narrows scope without contradicting.
Freestyling — Rapid collaborative short-response cycling among participants. Time-bounded, no single owner, round-robin turns.
Riffing — Solo divergent exploration from a seed contribution. May return to group with synthesis or spawn a new thread.

zed_layer.name: improve-codebase-architecture
zed_layer.description: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase. Use when the user wants to improve architecture, find refactoring opportunities, consolidate tightly-coupled modules, make a codebase more testable, or says 'this is a ball of mud'.

templates.description: Explore the codebase for architectural friction: shallow modules, tight coupling, missing locality, wide import surfaces, untested code. Apply the deletion test to suspected shallowness.
Present deepening candidates with files, problem, solution, benefits (leverage and locality), and recommendation strength. Use hKask domain vocabulary. Ask user which to explore.

Grill loop for a selected candidate: walk the design tree, define the deepened module shape, identify seams and adapters, confirm test surface. Update glossary and ADRs inline as decisions crystallize.

top_defects: arch-candidates.j2: energy_cap nested under contract (spec says top-level)
arch-candidates.j2: visibility nested under contract (spec says top-level)
arch-deepen.j2: energy_cap nested under contract (spec says top-level)

zed_layer.name: kata
zed_layer.description: Toyota Kata system — composes kata-starter, kata-improvement, and kata-coaching into a complete scientific capability development practice. Routes agents to the appropriate kata based on context: new agents start with kata-starter to build foundational habits, agents with specific capability gaps use kata-improvement, and coaches use kata-coaching to guide learners. Includes composition rules (starter→improvement transitions, improvement↔coaching switching), habit monitoring with CNS integration, iteration with variance assessment, carbon accounting, and OCAP consent enforcement. Use when deploying the full Toyota Kata methodology across a team of agents.

templates.description: Route agent to appropriate kata (starter/improvement/coaching/intervention).
Verify P2 consent then route to selected kata type.
Synthesize kata outcome with habit assessment (automaticity, streak, delta).
Generate encouraging intervention message when practice habit decays.
Determine if kata cycle should be re-run (variance, confidence, budget).
Compare iteration outputs for variance, consistency, and best run.
Check if kata composition switching is appropriate mid-session.

zed_layer.name: kata-coaching
zed_layer.description: Toyota Kata Coaching Kata — the 5-question dialogue for teaching scientific thinking. Used by managers, supervisors, and Curator to coach learners through the Improvement Kata. Five questions asked daily (~20 minutes) at the gemba: (1) What is the target condition? (2) What is the actual condition now? (3) What obstacles prevent reaching the target? Which ONE are you addressing? (4) What is your next step? What do you expect? (5) How quickly can we go and see what we learned? The coach provides procedural guidance, not solutions. Coaching is itself a skill that takes practice. Use when coaching an agent through an active Improvement Kata cycle.

templates.description: Full 5-question Coaching Kata dialogue cycle (~20 minutes).
Q1 — What is the Target Condition? Ground learner in their goal.
Q2 — What is the Actual Condition now? Ground learner in reality with data.
Q3 — What obstacles? Which ONE now? Force prioritization.
Q4 — What is your Next Step? What do you expect? Drive action with prediction.
Q5 — How quickly can we go and see? Close the feedback loop.

zed_layer.name: kata-improvement
zed_layer.description: Toyota Kata Improvement Kata — the 4-step scientific pattern for achieving challenging goals through iterative experimentation. Step 1: Understand the Direction (challenge from level above). Step 2: Grasp the Current Condition (facts and data, not assumptions). Step 3: Establish the Next Target Condition (measurable, 1 week to 3 months out, beyond current knowledge threshold). Step 4: Iterate toward the Target Condition through rapid PDCA experiments. Use when a specific, measurable capability gap exists. Prerequisite: complete kata-starter first. Pairs with kata-coaching for guided experimentation.

templates.description: Full 4-step Improvement Kata cycle with cybernetic feedback loop.
Step 1 — Understand the direction. Articulate challenge, target, measurement plan, knowledge threshold.
Step 2 — Grasp the current condition. Collect data, identify patterns, establish baseline metrics.
Step 3 — Establish next target condition. Declare measurable target, identify obstacles, pick ONE focus obstacle.
Step 4 — Experiment toward target. Design PDCA experiment with specific prediction and measurement plan.

zed_layer.name: kata-starter
zed_layer.description: Toyota Kata Starter practice routines for building foundational scientific thinking habits. Three deliberate practice routines: Five Questions Drill (practice asking the 5 Coaching Kata questions), PDCA Cycle (Plan-Do-Check-Act experimentation loop), and Observation Drill (distinguishing facts from interpretations). Use when an agent is new, has low automaticity scores, or needs to internalize scientific thinking before tackling specific capability gaps. Self-contained — no dependencies on other kata skills. 20 minutes a day is better than two hours once a week.

templates.description: Starter Kata practice cycle — select drill, practice, record, reflect.
Select appropriate starter drill based on history and automaticity.
Five Questions Drill — practice asking the 5 coaching questions in order.
PDCA Cycle Drill — practice Plan-Do-Check-Act on a trivial process.
Observation Drill — practice separating facts (IS) from interpretations (OUGHT).

zed_layer.name: magna-carta-verifier
zed_layer.description: Verifies that hKask's four Magna Carta principles (User Sovereignty, Affirmative Consent, Generative Space, Clear Boundaries) are correctly implemented and enforced. Uses YAML manifests to declare assertions per principle and Jinja2 templates to render verification procedures, reports, and test cases. Use when auditing sovereignty compliance, onboarding new resources, or verifying consent structures.

templates.description: Render a step-by-step verification procedure for each assertion in a Magna Carta principle manifest. For each assertion, produce the method (structural_audit, behavioral_probe, resource_verification, absence_check), targets, and expected pass/fail criteria.
Synthesize verification results into a structured report with per-principle status, per-assertion findings (pass/fail/warning), gap identification, and an overall compliance score.

Render Rust test case code blocks from assertion definitions. Each assertion produces a compilable test skeleton targeting the declared crate, module, and method with the appropriate verification method.

zed_layer.name: pragmatic-cybernetics

Use when diagnosing CNS alerts, analyzing feedback loop failures, evaluating variety deficits, or reasoning about the system's self-regulation architecture. Pairs with constraint-forces for enforcement-level decisions.

templates.description: Analyze a feedback loop on 5 properties: polarity, delay, gain, closure, fidelity. Diagnose broken loops and prescribe remediation.
Evaluate variety balance using Ashby's Law of Requisite Variety. Identify deficits, recommend attenuation or amplification strategies.
Map hKask components to VSM S1–S5. Assess system viability and identify unviable components requiring structural intervention.

zed_layer.name: pragmatic-laziness
zed_layer.description: Procedural composition skill. Finds the path of least action through meaning-space by orchestrating pragmatic-semantics, pragmatic-cybernetics, essentialist, and grill-me through a 3-phase lazy loop. Activate when the user says "be lazy about this", "find the lazy path", "least action", "decompose this", or "pragmatic laziness review".

templates.description: 3-phase lazy loop for least-action path finding. Sequences and delegates to other skills; does not add artifacts.
δS = 0 convergence check. Compares current and previous configurations to decide whether the lazy loop has reached a stationary point.

current_config.description: The current reduced configuration from the latest iteration

previous_config.description: The configuration from the previous iteration (empty on first iteration)

stationary.description: Whether δS = 0 — no further action reduction is possible

action_delta.description: negative = action decreased (progress); zero = stationary; positive = action increased (regression)

escalate.description: Whether to escalate to human (max iterations reached without convergence)

summary.description: What changed between iterations and why

situation.description: The design, artifact, or problem to find the least-action path for

context.description: Surrounding context: code, architecture, constraints, existing feedback loops

scope.description: full = all 3 phases; phase1_only = decompose only; phase2_only = identify loops only; phase3_only = find stationary action only

max_iterations.description: Maximum iterations of the full loop before escalation

stationary.description: Whether δS = 0 was reached (action is stationary)

escalation.description: Whether the loop escalated to human after exhausting iterations

report.description: Narrative summary of findings and reductions

templates.description: Classify a statement on both ontological (IS/OUGHT) and epistemic (declarative/probabilistic/subjunctive) axes. Determine its constraint force and provenance.

zed_layer.name: refactor-service-layer
zed_layer.description: Extract a shared service layer from duplicated surface logic (CLI, API, MCP servers) using the strangler fig pattern, deep-module discipline, and vertical tracer-bullet TDD. Activate when the user says "refactor service layer", "extract shared logic", "deduplicate surfaces", or when architectural analysis reveals business logic embedded in presentation layers.

templates.description: Audit and classify all duplicated operations across CLI, API, and MCP surfaces. Apply the deletion test to each candidate. Produce RDF triples, classification table, and mermaid entity-relationship diagram of the duplication landscape.

Execute the strangler fig migration for a selected domain: create service operation, wire CLI, wire API, delete duplication, verify. Enforces one-domain- per-commit discipline, dependency direction checks, and surgical change scope.

Verify surgical completeness after a domain migration or full extraction: dependency direction, depth test, P6/P7/P8 compliance, clippy, test suite, deletion test on service modules. Produces a structured pass/fail report.

zed_layer.name: rust-expertise
zed_layer.description: Idiomatic Rust design and implementation through the lens of Graydon Hoare's programming philosophy. Type-driven design, ownership as architecture, fearless refactoring, zero-cost abstraction, and the compiler as design partner. Covers type design, ownership patterns, error handling, unsafe boundaries, idiom auditing, and refactoring toward deeper modules.

templates.description: Assess a Rust design problem against the eight philosophical foundations (type-driven design, ownership as architecture, fearless refactoring, zero-cost abstraction, explicit over implicit, composition over inheritance, error as values, unsafe as contract). Surfaces invariants, invalid states, ownership relationships, error domains, and safety boundaries.

Design Rust types that make invalid states unrepresentable. Choose type kind (enum/struct/newtype), push validation to construction, minimise public API, derive strategically, and implement traits for capability rather than taxonomy.

Architect ownership and borrowing patterns for Rust data structures. Default to sole ownership, introduce sharing only when the ownership DAG demands it, prefer borrowed parameters, use lifetimes as documentation, and treat interior mutability as a deliberate relaxation.

Design error types as first-class citizens. Typed errors for library code (thiserror), opaque errors for application boundaries (anyhow), error variants that carry context, propagation with ?, and deliberate handling at every level.

Audit existing Rust code for idiomatic compliance across six dimensions: type strength, ownership clarity, error handling hygiene, trait usage, unsafe audit, and module depth. Produces a violations report with severity scores and corrective recommendations.

Design and audit unsafe code boundaries. Minimise the unsafe surface, document safety invariants, prefer unsafe fn over unsafe blocks, ensure every unsafe block is auditable in isolation, and verify that safe abstractions correctly encapsulate unsafe operations.

Plan idiomatic refactoring of non-idiomatic Rust toward deeper modules and stronger types. Extract newtypes, replace booleans with enums, consolidate unsafe, strengthen error types, simplify ownership, and deepen shallow modules.

top_defects: rust-error-design.j2: energy_cap nested under contract (spec says top-level)
rust-error-design.j2: visibility nested under contract (spec says top-level)
rust-idiom-audit.j2: energy_cap nested under contract (spec says top-level)

zed_layer.name: skill-translator
zed_layer.description: Translate agent skills into hKask's dual-layer architecture: registry crate (manifest.yaml + .j2 templates) as primary runtime artifact, plus SKILL.md companion guide for the Zed coding agent. Analyze source format, classify steps, map to both layers. Use when adapting skills from other projects, converting between skill systems, or when the user says 'translate this skill' or 'convert this skill'.

templates.description: Analyze a source skill's format on six dimensions (manifest type, execution model, state model, consent model, observability, domain references). Classify each step as cognitive, workflow, or guardrail. Determine template_type per step. Assess dual-layer translation feasibility.

Translate a classified skill from source format to hKask dual-layer format
  (registry crate + SKILL.md companion).
Map source elements to hKask equivalents, drop concepts with no equivalent, produce validated output with translation summary.

zed_layer.name: strangler-fig
zed_layer.description: Incremental architectural migration via Martin Fowler's Strangler Fig pattern. Introduce new implementation alongside old, migrate one domain at a time, both paths delegate before any deletion. System fully functional at every intermediate step. Use when migrating architecture, replacing legacy code, or extracting a service layer.

templates.description: Map domains, identify consumers, classify overlap (Identical/Divergent/ Surface-only), and sequence migration by risk. Produce a prioritized migration plan with per-domain CREATE→WIRE→DELETE steps.

Execute one domain migration step: create new component, wire one consumer at a time, verify each consumer, delete old code only after full wiring. Produces a step completion report with verification results.
Verify system functional at intermediate migration step. Run workspace build + test + lint, detect regressions, confirm old path intact until deletion, verify reversibility of current step.

zed_layer.name: tdd
zed_layer.description: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when building features or fixing bugs with TDD, mentions 'red-green-refactor', wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.

templates.description: Plan a TDD cycle: extract functional requirements from specifications, identify public interfaces, prioritize behaviors by risk (P0-P2+), and get user approval before writing any code.
Execute a tracer bullet: write ONE failing test for ONE behavior anchored to a spec requirement via spec_id (from spec/graph/query), then write the minimal code to make it pass.
Refactor while GREEN: extract duplication, deepen modules, apply SOLID principles. Preserve // REQ: tags during refactoring. Verify tests still pass after each refactor step.

Verify TDD cycle completion: all tests pass, clippy clean, no todo!/unimplemented! stubs, tests describe behavior not implementation, spec traceability via // REQ: tags, functional coverage gaps identified.

Functional gap analysis: compare specification requirements against tested behaviors. Identify uncovered requirements (gaps) and produce deferral recommendations for OPEN_QUESTIONS.md. P0 gaps MUST have tracer bullets.

zed_layer.description: Zoom out to give broader context or a higher-level perspective on unfamiliar code. Use when you're unfamiliar with a section of code, need to understand how it fits into the bigger picture, or when you're lost in the weeds.

templates.description: Generate a module map, caller graph, data flow summary, boundary summary, and key invariants for the current code region. Uses hKask domain vocabulary.
